concept: Bag / Flexible Container for Carrying or Keeping
description: A flexible container with an opening, used to carry or keep objects. Cloth, leather, fiber, or another yielding material lets a bag change shape around what it holds. 'molu' (container) is general, while empty 'lirupa' folds nearly flat.
sound_symbolism: 'Liru' (fabric) survives intact. 'Pa' opens like the mouth through which things are put and taken.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia mua ha lirupa nuora phelu.' (I keep food in this bag.) Ownership and material fit before the noun. 'wiru' (basket) is woven and self-supporting; a bag collapses around its contents.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A mended reusable bag keeps its usefulness through many errands instead of becoming single-use wrapping.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sacks and pouches moved seed, flour, tools, and personal goods while folding almost flat for the return journey.

tags.creation: a flexible container made from cloth, leather, or fiber
tags.physical: a soft-sided holder that changes shape around its contents
